request.question: Does Empire of the Sun or 3 Doors Down have more band members?

title: Empire of the Sun (band)
Bigger Than Life Tour
3 Doors Down (album)
Citizen/Soldier
Away from the Sun
3 Doors Down
Here Without You
When I'm Gone (3 Doors Down song)
The Doors discography
Seventeen Days
sentences: Empire of the Sun is an Australian electronic music duo from Sydney, formed in 2007.
The band is a collaboration between Luke Steele of alternative rock act The Sleepy Jackson, and Nick Littlemore of electronic dance outfit Pnau.
Their 2008 debut album "Walking on a Dream" brought the duo international success and has been certified double platinum in Australia and gold in the United Kingdom.

The album provided a number of internationally charting singles including "Walking on a Dream" which peaked at number ten on the Australian ARIA Singles Chart and reached number sixty four on the UK Singles Chart, and "We Are the People", which peaked at number fourteen on the UK Singles Chart.
The Bigger Than Life Tour was a co- headlining tour by American rock bands, Daughtry and 3 Doors Down.
The tour supported Daughtry's third studio album, "Break the Spell", and 3 Doors Down's "The Greatest Hits" album.
3 Doors Down is the self-titled fourth studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on May 20, 2008. "
It's Not My Time" was the first single from the album and was released in February 2008.
It can be heard on the band's MySpace site.
Another song on the album, "Citizen/Soldier" was released in 2007 as a tribute to the National Guard.
"Citizen/Soldier" is a single by the American alternative rock band 3 Doors Down from their self-titled album "3 Doors Down".
The song was released as a single in November 2007 in conjunction with a recruitment campaign by the United States National Guard.
The lyrics convey the band's views regarding the actions performed by the Guard.
It was released as the third single for American active rock and mainstream rock radio stations in November 2008, while it is their fourth overall single.
Away from the Sun is the second studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on November 12, 2002.
The singles released for the album were as follows: "When I'm Gone", "The Road I'm On", "Here Without You" and "Away from the Sun".
The recording sessions took place during the summer of 2002 with Producer/Engineer Rick Parashar at London Bridge Studios, in Seattle, Washington.
Rush guitarist Alex Lifeson performed live with the band during their premiere party in Biloxi for "Away from the Sun".
The album has sold four million copies worldwide, including well over three million in the US alone.
3 Doors Down is an American rock band from Escatawpa, Mississippi, that formed in 1996.
The band originally consisted of Brad Arnold (lead vocals/drums), Todd Harrell (bass), and Matt Roberts (lead guitar, backing vocals).
They were soon joined by rhythm guitarist Chris Henderson.
The band rose to international fame with their first single, "Kryptonite", which charted in the top three on the "Billboard" Hot 100 chart.
The band then signed with Republic Records and released their debut album, "The Better Life", in 2000.
The album was the 11th-best-selling album of the year and was certified 6x platinum in the United States.
They were later joined by drummer Richard Liles, who played during the tour for their first album.
"Here Without You" is a song by American rock band 3 Doors Down.
It was released in August 2003 as the third single from the album "Away from the Sun".
It peaked at No. 5 on the "Billboard" Hot 100, for the week ending November 8, 2003.
Only their songs "Kryptonite" and "When I'm Gone" reached higher positions on the chart, peaking at No. 3 and No. 4, respectively.
It has since been certified 2x Platinum in the United States and Platinum in Australia.
The song was a large success on pop radio, becoming their third No. 1 hit on the Mainstream Top 40 chart and their first No. 1 on the Adult Top 40 chart, where it stayed at the top for 13 weeks.
"When I'm Gone" is a song by American rock band 3 Doors Down.
It was released in October 2002 as the lead single from their second album, "Away from the Sun".
It peaked at  4 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 chart in April 2003.
The song also spent seventeen weeks atop the "Billboard" Hot Mainstream Rock Tracks chart from November 2002 through March 2003, becoming one of the longest-running number-one singles on that chart.
The song also peaked  2 on the Hot Modern Rock Tracks chart almost reaching  1 by one spot from Queens of the Stone Age's "No One Knows" on the issue dated February 15, 2003.
It spent one week atop the Top 40 Mainstream chart for one week on May 3, 2003 and also peaked at  3 on the Adult Top 40 chart.
The following is the discography of the American rock band The Doors.
Formed in Los Angeles in 1965, the group consisted of Jim Morrison (vocals), Ray Manzarek (keyboards), John Densmore (drums), and Robby Krieger (guitar).
The Doors became one of the most popular rock bands of their era.
Their debut album, "The Doors" (1967), released by Elektra Records, charted at No. 2 on the US "Billboard" 200 and produced the group's most successful single, "Light My Fire".

The album received several sales certifications including a 4 times multi-platinum from both the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA), and from the Canadian Recording Industry Association (CRIA).

The Doors' second studio album, "Strange Days" (1967), often recognized as their most creative output, failed to produce a hit single as popular as "Light My Fire", though the album sold well commercially but did not reach the same level of success as the debut.
It reached No. 3 on the "Billboard" 200 and was certified platinum in the United States and Canada.
The Doors' third studio album "Waiting for the Sun" (1968), was regarded as an artistic disappointment when compared to their earlier material.
However, commercially it was very successful and reached No. 1 in the US and France, and produced their second No. 1 single, "Hello, I Love You".
"Waiting for the Sun" was the first Doors album to chart in the United Kingdom, where it peaked inside the Top 20.
The album was certified gold in that country by the British Phonographic Industry (BPI), as well as being certified gold and platinum in several other countries.
Seventeen Days is the third studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on February 8, 2005, five years to the day that 3 Doors Down released their debut album, "The Better Life".

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: Does Empire of the Sun or 3 Doors Down have more band members?
hop1_docs: Another 700 Miles | Another 700 Miles is a live EP by 3 Doors Down released on November 11, 2003.  It was recorded live at a performance in Chicago, Illinois during the band's Away from the Sun tour.

High Tyde | High Tyde are an English indie pop quartet from Brighton, England.  The ages of the band members range from 18 to 19.  They have played at major music festivals, such as Boardmasters, Dot 2 Dot, Underground, Fieldview, Reading, and Y Not.  They have also played support shows for Little Comets, Bad Suns, Young Kato, and Peace.  They have been featured on BBC Radio 1.  The sound of the band is inspired from indie bands like Two Door Cinema Club and Foals.  Their sound also has similarities of bands like The 1975 and Bombay Bicycle Club.  Their music gives off a summer vibe.

Away from the Sun | Away from the Sun is the second studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on November 12, 2002.  The singles released for the album were as follows: "When I'm Gone", "The Road I'm On", "Here Without You" and "Away from the Sun".  The recording sessions took place during the summer of 2002 with Producer/Engineer Rick Parashar at London Bridge Studios, in Seattle, Washington.  Rush guitarist Alex Lifeson performed live with the band during their premiere party in Biloxi for "Away from the Sun".  The album has sold four million copies worldwide, including well over three million in the US alone.

3 Doors Down (album) | 3 Doors Down is the self-titled fourth studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on May 20, 2008. " It's Not My Time" was the first single from the album and was released in February 2008.  It can be heard on the band's MySpace site.  Another song on the album, "Citizen/Soldier" was released in 2007 as a tribute to the National Guard.

The Road I'm On | "The Road I'm On" is a song recorded by American rock band 3 Doors Down.  It was released in April 2003 as the second single from their second album, "Away from the Sun".  The song reached number 8 on the U.S. "Billboard" Mainstream Rock charts and number 24 on the U.S. "Billboard" Modern Rock charts.

Supralateral arc | A supralateral arc is a comparatively rare member of the halo family which in its complete form appears as a large, faintly rainbow-colored band in a wide arc above the sun and appearing to encircle it, at about twice the distance as the familiar 22° halo.  In reality, however, the supralateral arc does not form a circle and never reaches below the sun.  When present, the supralateral arc touches the (much more common) circumzenithal arc from below.  As in all colored halos, the arc has its red side directed towards the sun, its blue part away from it.

Walking on a Dream (song) | "Walking on a Dream" is a song by Australian electronic music duo Empire of the Sun, which was released on 30 August 2008 as the lead single ahead of their debut album of the same name (3 October 2008).  The track was co-written by band members, Luke Steele and Nick Littlemore with Jonathan "Donnie" Sloan.  The full Sam La More remix was made available as a free download on the band's official website.
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found:
  - Empire of the Sun: Passage [7] calls them an "Australian electronic music duo" and notes the song was "co-written by band members, Luke Steele and Nick Littlemore" (passage [7]).
  - 3 Doors Down: Passages [1], [3], and [4] refer to the band via album/EP pages (e.g., "Another 700 Miles", "Away from the Sun", "3 Doors Down (album)") and use the token "American rock band 3 Doors Down" (passage [3]), but none list a band member count or current lineup.
- Ambiguities:
  - For 3 Doors Down, only album/EP pages appear in the provided passages ("Another 700 Miles" [1], "Away from the Sun" [3], "3 Doors Down (album)" [4]); the main "3 Doors Down" band page or a members/lineup section is not present in the passages.
  - For Empire of the Sun, we have only a song page mention (passage [7]) rather than the band's main page; however, the passage explicitly calls them a "duo".
- Bridge entity / Missing fact:
  - Bridge entities: "Empire of the Sun" and "3 Doors Down" (as named in the passages).
  - Precise missing fact needed: the number of band members (current lineup count) for 3 Doors Down — and a confirmation of Empire of the Sun's member count on the band's main page (to verify the duo status in the lede/infobox).
- Retrieval direction (prioritized):
  1) Open the "3 Doors Down" main page (or "3 Doors Down (band)") — check the lede/infobox and the "Members", "Band members", "Line-up", or "History" sections to find the current number of members and list of current members. Search keywords: "3 Doors Down members", "3 Doors Down band members list".
  2) Open the "Empire of the Sun" main page — check the lede/infobox and "Members" or "Band members" section to confirm member count and verify Luke Steele and Nick Littlemore as members. Search keywords: "Empire of the Sun members", "Empire of the Sun Luke Steele Nick Littlemore".
  3) If the 3 Doors Down main page is not found, open relevant disambiguation or official band pages and check band biography sections (lede/infobox and "History"/"Members") or reputable discography/lineup sources.
Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved.
response.hop2_query: 3 Doors Down members Empire of the Sun members
hop2_docs: The Sun Goes Down (Living It Up) | "The Sun Goes Down (Living It Up)" is a single released in 1983 by the musical group Level 42.  It is one of the few songs by the group which featured Mike Lindup on lead vocals (although Mark King did contribute as lead vocalist on this track).  After the success of "The Chinese Way", and of the album "The Pursuit of Accidents", this song featured on the newly released "Standing in the Light" album.  The first single from the album, "Out Of Sight, Out Of Mind", did not obtain the expected success for the recording and for the members of the band, leaving the whole expectation for the second single, The Sun Goes Down.

Two Door Cinema Club | Two Door Cinema Club are an Irish indie rock band from Bangor and Donaghadee in County Down, Northern Ireland.  The band formed in 2007 and is composed of three members: Alex Trimble (vocals, rhythm guitar, beats, synths), Sam Halliday (lead guitar, backing vocals), and Kevin Baird (bass, synths, backing vocals).
Another 700 Miles | Another 700 Miles is a live EP by 3 Doors Down released on November 11, 2003.  It was recorded live at a performance in Chicago, Illinois during the band's Away from the Sun tour.

Passiena (gens) | The gens Passiena, occasionally written Passienia, Passenia, Passennia, or Passenna, was a plebeian family at Rome, originally of equestrian rank, but at least one member was later admitted to the patriciate.  Members of this gens appear in history from the early years of the Empire down to the third century, and several obtained the consulship, beginning with Lucius Passienus Rufus in 4 BC.

Luke Steele (musician) | Luke James Steele (born 13 December 1979) is an Australian musician, singer and songwriter.  Steele is the vocalist and primary songwriter of the alternative rock band The Sleepy Jackson and is a member of the electronic music duo Empire of the Sun.

Away from the Sun | Away from the Sun is the second studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on November 12, 2002.  The singles released for the album were as follows: "When I'm Gone", "The Road I'm On", "Here Without You" and "Away from the Sun".  The recording sessions took place during the summer of 2002 with Producer/Engineer Rick Parashar at London Bridge Studios, in Seattle, Washington.  Rush guitarist Alex Lifeson performed live with the band during their premiere party in Biloxi for "Away from the Sun".  The album has sold four million copies worldwide, including well over three million in the US alone.

Jean-Pierre Door | Jean-Pierre Door (born April 1, 1942 in Sully-sur-Loire) is a member of the National Assembly of France.  He represents the Loiret department, and is a member of the The Republicans.

response.summary_2: Empire of the Sun are an Australian electronic music duo. 3 Doors Down are an American rock band, but the provided passages (album pages) do not specify the band's number of members or current lineup.
response.answer: Cannot determine from summaries; Empire of the Sun are a duo but 3 Doors Down's member count is not provided.
